question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns the Topic's timestampRange (lower bound = creation/last-update time) to correlate topic key material with on-chain transaction history, identifying the exact transaction that set the current adminKey and using that information to reconstruct the key derivation path for unauthorized signing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to retrieve a Topic object where submitKey is a KeyList (multiple keys), read all keys in the list from the protobuf-encoded bytes, and determine that only one of the keys needs to sign, then target the weakest key in the list to forge submit authorization without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any IP-based or session-based rate limiting on findById() to perform a bulk harvest of all topic adminKey and submitKey values across the entire network, creating a comprehensive key registry that enables systematic bypass of signing requirements for any topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the feeExemptKeyList of a topic, find that it contains a well-known or widely-used public key (e.g., a system account key), and then submit ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions that claim fee exemption under that key without the key owner's approval, bypassing custom fee collection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not check whether the Topic is deleted (deleted flag is in the Entity table, not the Topic table) before returning key material, allowing them to read keys of deleted topics and potentially replay old signed transactions that were authorized under those keys?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() with an EntityId whose getId() value is crafted to overflow or underflow the shard/realm/num bit fields in EntityId.encode(), causing the service to look up a different topic ID than intended and return key material for a topic the attacker should not access, enabling unauthorized transaction signing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the adminKey of a topic, determine that it is a contract-controlled key (smart contract ID key), and then interact with the controlling smart contract directly to trigger admin operations on the topic without the contract owner's explicit approval for that specific topic action?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns the same Topic object for both the current state and any cached state, and use stale cached key material (from the EntityId Caffeine cache with 60-minute expiry) to authorize transactions using an old key that has since been revoked by the topic owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the submitKey of a topic, find that it is a threshold key with a 1-of-N structure, and then use the exposed list of N public keys to identify the account with the weakest security posture (e.g., a known compromised account) and submit messages using that account's key without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() exposes the feeScheduleKey bytes, decode the protobuf to identify the key type and value, and then use that information to submit a ConsensusUpdateTopic transaction with a modified fee schedule, bypassing the requirement for feeScheduleKey owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the adminKey of a topic and compare it against the adminKey of other topics returned by the same endpoint, identifying topics that share the same adminKey, and then use a single compromised key to authorize admin transactions across multiple topics without each topic owner's individual approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex (^((\\d{1,4})\\.)?((\\d{1,5})\\.)?(\\d{1,12})$) to pass a topic ID that parses to a different shard/realm combination than intended, causing findById() to return key material for a topic in a different shard/realm without that shard/realm's authorization controls being applied?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the adminKey bytes of a topic, use the ProtobufEncoded key type to decode the raw bytes, and identify that the key is an ECDSA_SECP256K1 key, then use the exposed public key to derive the Ethereum-compatible address and target the key owner for a private key extraction attack to bypass signing requirements?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not log or audit access to sensitive topic key material, allowing them to silently harvest adminKey and submitKey bytes from thousands of topics without triggering any security alerts, enabling large-scale unauthorized transaction authorization bypass?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the feeExemptKeyList of a topic, find that it contains a key that matches the submitKey, and then submit messages to the topic claiming fee exemption under the submitKey, bypassing custom fee payment without the topic owner's approval for fee exemption?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns the Topic's createdTimestamp, correlate it with the block explorer to find the ConsensusCreateTopic transaction, extract the original key material from the transaction body, and use that information to identify if the current adminKey was derived from a predictable seed, enabling unauthorized signing without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the submitKey of a topic, find that it is an ED25519 key, and use the exposed 32-byte public key to perform a preimage attack or brute-force the corresponding private key if the key was generated with insufficient entropy, enabling unauthorized message submission without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any CORS restriction or authentication on the /api/v1/topics/{id} endpoint backed by findById(), to make cross-origin requests from a malicious web page that harvests topic key material from authenticated users' browsers, enabling the attacker to collect keys and authorize transactions without the key owners' approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the adminKey of a topic, find that it is a KeyList with multiple keys, and then use the exposed key list to identify which accounts are authorized admins, targeting those accounts for phishing or social engineering attacks to obtain their private keys and authorize admin transactions without the legitimate owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not differentiate between a topic with an empty feeExemptKeyList (serialized as empty protobuf) and a topic with no feeExemptKeyList (null), causing the service to incorrectly indicate fee exemption status and allowing the attacker to submit messages without paying fees without the topic owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the adminKey of a topic, determine that it is a contract key pointing to a specific smart contract, and then call that smart contract's public functions to trigger a ConsensusUpdateTopic transaction on behalf of the contract, bypassing the requirement for the contract owner's explicit approval for that specific topic modification?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns the Topic object with all key fields populated from the database without any masking or redaction, allowing them to read the full protobuf-encoded feeScheduleKey and use it to identify the key owner's account, then target that account to bypass fee schedule update authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() with a topic ID that exists in the topic_history table but not the current topic table, and if the repository query accidentally returns historical data, read stale key material that was previously revoked, using it to authorize transactions that should no longer be valid without the current owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() does not validate that the EntityId passed to it corresponds to an entity of type TOPIC (the type check is only in the entity table, not the topic table), allowing them to pass an EntityId that maps to a non-topic entity's ID and retrieve whatever key material happens to be stored in the topic table for that ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user call findById() to read the submitKey of a topic, find that it is a threshold key requiring M-of-N signatures, and use the exposed N public keys to identify all authorized submitters, then target the M weakest key holders to obtain their private keys and submit messages without the full set of required signers' approval?
